summary.intent: Add 100+ default bundled skills to Moltis by copying vetted skills from Hermes Agent and OpenClaw, with category UI, format fallback, and proper metadata.
summary.outcome: 101 bundled skills embedded in the binary via include_dir!, with category metadata, web UI category filter, system prompt format fallback, origin tracking, requires: frontmatter, and all PR review issues resolved.

repo: Moltis uses include_dir! for embedding static assets at compile time — same pattern for web UI HTML/CSS and now skills
SkillSource enum in crates/skills/src/types.rs drives priority: Project > Personal > Registry > Plugin > Bundled
Skill discovery is composed via SkillDiscoverer trait in crates/skills/src/discover.rs — new sources plug in cleanly
Web UI /api/skills endpoint in crates/web/src/api.rs creates its own FsSkillDiscoverer independently from gateway — bundled source must be wired separately
Skills system already had requires: frontmatter with bins/any_bins/install fields — Hermes/OpenClaw skills needed to be mapped to this format
SkillSource exhaustive match also exists in crates/skills/src/watcher.rs — must add Bundled arm (no-op) when adding new variant
Directory name must match SKILL.md frontmatter name field — read_skill() searches by directory name, causing silent failures when they diverge
gateway and web crates need bundled-skills feature forwarded from cli/Cargo.toml separately

code.finding: BundledSkillStore with include_dir! embedding, dev-mode filesystem fallback, recursive directory traversal for nested categories like mlops/training/axolotl
CompositeSkillDiscoverer merges FS + bundled results with name deduplication using seen.insert() — must deduplicate within bundled set too, not just across sources
Three-tier format fallback: full (name+desc+attrs) → compact (self-closing, no desc) → truncated (binary search prefix). Header text changes to signal format tier.
read_bundled() must call normalize_relative_skill_file_path() before passing to store — same normalization as FS path to prevent traversal in dev mode
api_skills_handler creates its own FsSkillDiscoverer with only Personal+Project — must be updated to CompositeSkillDiscoverer for bundled skills to appear in web UI
CDN script tag needed SRI integrity hash — CodeQL flagged missing integrity attribute

workflow: Python script with sed-like string manipulation is more reliable than shell sed for multi-line YAML frontmatter injection
Cargo check is sufficient to validate feature flag wiring across crates without running full test suite
Greptile automated review caught real issues (godmode skill bundled by default, path traversal) — worth waiting for before merging
Directory/name mismatches in vendored skills are caught by a test that calls read_skill() on every discovered skill name — add this test when ingesting external skill sets
When copying skills from external repos, run cleanup in this order: (1) copy raw, (2) inject origin metadata, (3) clean frontmatter, (4) clean body text refs
GraphQL mutations via gh api work for resolving PR review threads programmatically — use pullRequestReviewThread resolver with id from listReviews query

friction: Shell sed truncated SKILL.md files during origin metadata injection — had to re-copy and use Python instead
Background bash tasks hung silently, requiring manual re-runs of comparison and polling commands
Disk space exhausted on worktree during gateway test run (llama-cpp compilation) — couldn't run full integration tests
include_dir! recursive discovery required updating both FS and embedded code paths separately — easy to miss one
Six skills had directory/name mismatches (e.g., trl-fine-tuning/ vs name: fine-tuning-with-trl) causing silent read failures — only caught by tests
CLI, gateway, and web crates each need bundled-skills feature forwarded independently — no single place to wire it
open_items: Hermes-specific body text remains in godmode/SKILL.md (intentionally kept as Nous Hermes model references)
GitHub skills still reference ~/.hermes/.env token lookup patterns in some edge cases
No compact fallback threshold configured — default 30KB budget is generous, may need tuning as skill count grows beyond ~400
OpenClaw extensions (Feishu, QQBot, ACP integrations) not ported — could be useful for users with those platforms
Vendored Python sidecar scripts still reference Hermes paths in some skills — mechanical cleanup deferred
No UI to show origin/provenance of bundled skills in web UI — category filter added but source attribution not surfaced
